## OVERVIEW

The $5M Sales Letter Formula is a workshop-format program where Jon Benson reverse-engineers the sales page that generated $5M+ in revenue. It covers the exact 18-subheadline structure, the Duality Marketing concept, email mastery with 21 specific tricks, and the Samurai Code of copywriting ethics. This source bridges Jon's VSL expertise with traditional sales page and email copywriting.

---

| 2 | Email Mastery: 21 Email Tricks | Email copywriting system |
| 3 | Duality Marketing & Trifecta System | Gain/loss framing + 3-pillar copy |
| 4 | Article Page Formula & Parting Gift Close | Native-style sales pages + lead capture for non-buyers |
| 5 | Samurai Code & Personal Branding | Ethics framework + authenticity in copy |

## KEY FRAMEWORKS

| Framework | Description | Layer |
|-----------|-------------|-------|
| 18-Subheadline Sales Page Formula | Each subheadline functions as an independent mini-hook | L4 |
| 4 E's of Proof | Experience → Evidence → Endorsements → Examples (in order of impact) | L4 |
| Article Page Formula | Sales page disguised as editorial article for cold traffic | L4 |
| Trifecta | Passion + Personality + Persuasion — the 3 pillars of connecting copy | L4 |
| Parting Gift Close | Free valuable offer at end for non-buyers — captures lead for remarketing | L5 |
| 21 Email Tricks | Complete email copywriting system (subject lines, preview text, PS strategy) | L5 |
| Duality Marketing | Show both sides (what you gain vs what you lose) to double emotional urgency | L2 |
| Samurai Code | 7 principles of copywriting ethics | L1 |

---

## KEY HEURISTICS

| Heuristic | Value | Domain |
|-----------|-------|--------|
| Mobile-first | 70%+ traffic is mobile — write for mobile first | mobile-marketing |
| Avatar definition | Psychographics over demographics — what they FEEL at 3am | avatar-strategy |
| Personal branding in copy | Real name, real photo, real story — anonymity kills trust | personal-branding |
| NLP in email | Exactly 1 action drop + 1 open loop per email | email-marketing |
| Price anchoring | Anchored price ($2997 crossed to $997) works 3x better than broken price ($997) | pricing-psychology |
| Subject line odd numbers | Odd numbers in email subject lines outperform even | email-marketing |
| PS link strategy | PS should contain different link/angle than body CTA | email-marketing |
